Yeah, yeah, feeding them.

Okay, yeah, it's a process for sure.

Yeah, and I'm excited that you'll be in town next week.

So yeah, it should be good, meeting quite a lot of people over there.
Yeah, have some, like, quite a few meetings I'm over there for.
So yeah, but Monday is, like, pretty much free.
So I think we can just find whatever time works for us all.
Okay, awesome.
Yeah, yeah, like, you could either come to the office or George and I could meet you closer to where you're staying.
Yeah, we're super flexible.
So yeah.
Hey, Johanna, how are you doing?
We are muted.
Oh, sorry.
Hey, Johanna, how are you doing?
I'm good.
How are you?
Good, good.
Are you also going to be in town next week or are you?
Okay.
Not me.
I wish.
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, we have some folks in town this week, so hence why I'm in the office today.
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah.
So just to jump in, Sam, I know you had questions about the publishing cadence.
So, okay.
Number one.
So while I know that in the contract it stated 10 and 10, but for the guide specifically, I was under the impression that we were still like that 10 would be steady state.
But that we were still going to do the 25 a week until we like hit those 380 keywords.
And then we would, the steady state would be 10 a week.
But it's like basically the way that the contract.
The way it is, is because you're still ultimately only paying for the 10 a week, but for a certain period of time, we'll be doing the 25.
That said, if you don't want us to be doing the 25, just let us know.
We don't have to be doing that, but the idea was that there were some good keyword opportunities that we wanted to quickly get to.

Just so I understand that, so say we do, let's just say we did 25 a week for two weeks, it would be 50 in total, and then that would, we would then not produce for like three weeks at the, oh sorry, sorry, I got my numbers mixed up.
So say we did 25 a week for two months, and it's like, that's what, nine weeks, so 90, oh sorry, it would be 225 new guides.

Um, the, but we're paying for 10 a week, so then we just like go through a period after that initial 225 where you just, where we would continue paying and you just wouldn't produce any, is that right, or am I missing it?
no, no, no, then we would just go to 10 a week, to the 10 guides a week.
Okay.
Once we get.
Yeah.
So we're paying for 10, but you're doing like 25 a week.
Right.
Okay.
That's fine with that.
That's fine with me.
Yeah, it's, yeah, it was, no, I get that it's confusing.
It was, yeah, um, it was just what, what Marcel was thinking.
And so, I mean, he's the boss.
We were like, yeah, let's, let's, let's do that.
Um, yeah.
So you're paying for 10.
For a couple months, you're going to be getting 25 a week.
Um, once we get through those keywords, then you're.
You're just getting what you're paying for.
Regarding the templates.
Oh, yeah.
No, exactly.
was going to ask.
Okay.
Yeah.
So regarding the templates.
So you're paying for the 10 a week.
We did hit a bit of a backlog last week, but I just checked in with engineering today and they will be able to get those remaining five from last week published this week.
I think something in their publishing workflow just got messed up.
But those templates are all ready to go, but it's just a matter of publishing them.
So those will definitely be published within the next couple of days and then we're still on track to hit the additional 10 by the end of this week.
I think for me, my main action item is just needing to kind of be on engineering about making sure that they're like staying on track of the content that they're getting from.

Sounds great.
Thanks, Megan.

haven't.

And on that note, I was curious, like, if...

So I guess I'm curious like what our baseline is or should be or if like is December a good baseline to have like or do you feel like there may be more people like building on Lovable in December because like maybe people are on vacation and then like on the holidays or like or I guess like even like what you were seeing on like into like internal analytics.
Like were you seeing that like people were maybe building less in January or was that actually maybe just specific to the templates?
Does that make sense?
Yeah, it does.
And it's definitely specific to the templates.
We're seeing increased activity in January versus December.
So I think the baseline for December is fine.
It does look like there was a real spike in from like templates and guides purchases in the run up to Christmas.
Right, right.
Yeah, I think, I don't think there's any changes to the data, but.
That's.
There is, but yeah, like, there's no reason for us to think that, as long as Google Analytics hasn't changed, like, there's no reason for us to think that there should be less transactions.
Yeah, okay.
Okay, for sure.
Then, I guess my other thought is, maybe it's a matter of, let me scroll down to some of the better performing templates.
So, like, yeah, so I have it broken down by, like, all sources versus just organic referral.
And so, the hub page is always doing the best, but then it's, like, we have these landing pages, so it's, like, maybe we need to do more landing pages or, like, more e-commerce templates.
Like, it's possible that we might need to fine-tune the exact...
templates that we are building.

And, and yeah, and I think maybe, yeah, would it, yeah, I'm still curious, like, would it be possible to get a sense of, like, on the Lovable platform right now, it's like, do you have data around what people are, what types of things, like, people are building, and then we can sort of decide, like, oh, like, these are maybe some of the templates that we should build for them.
Because, like, right now, we're, our template strategy is pretty rooted in, in SEO, but I'm wondering if that necessarily translates to what people are actually building on Lovable.
Yeah, it's a really good question.
I agree.
I think we should, we should find a balance where there's conversion plus, plus SEO benefit.
Yeah.
I guess that.
And we're really looking for here is templates that would need more than just a free account to build.

And the reason I say that is like a portfolio, I imagine that someone can do it with like the five free credits per day that they get and can sort of like over the course of a week getting those five free credits a day.
Probably achieve quite a lot there.
That's fair.
An e-commerce store, you might want to, you would want to have like a full-on subscription.
Right.
Yeah, so like those more robust projects where it's not just like a one and done and it's like pretty, pretty simple to achieve.
Can you add a like action for myself to figure that out for you?
Yeah, yeah, for sure.
Yeah, because I think that would...
That would definitely be helpful.
Okay.
I'm just taking this in a different path.
Okay.
Okay.
Going back.
Yeah.
So, I mean, that's sort of the high level.
Like, I can also, like, dive in a little bit deeper.
But also, would you, so we have a new LLM, like, analysis platform called Check That, that is, we're just kind of getting up and running.
And that's where you can kind of track your visibility in comparison to other competitors.
And it's essentially our version of Scrunch, but that's what we're using now to monitor AI visibility.
But before I do that, is there anything else in here that you kind of want me to do?
Nothing from my side.
Yana, are you all right?
You were good?
Yeah, nothing specific.
I'm actually, no.
Okay.
Okay, cool.
Okay.
So, this is, check that, and you, I will be able to get you access to this in the next couple of days.
We're just, the team is still just like working on permissions.
But so, just like Scrunch, we are tracking prompts.
We're tracking a mix of brands.
Branded and non-branded.
Let's see.
Okay, let's see if that'll load.
Okay, here we go.

Yeah, so this is, these are the prompts, but yeah, we can filter out by all prompts, branded versus non-branded, which isn't necessarily perfect because we're still seeing some of that, but then you'll see like Best AI App Builders 2025.
Best Vibe Coding Tools.
So definitely a mix there.
And so what we're seeing though so far, I think this has actually been set up for a little bit now, so there's more data than some of our other accounts.
But, well, yeah, I guess the first thing I want to say though is that the visibility data is really only as good as the prompts that we're tracking.
Okay?
All

So I'm still, I want to, I need to dive in a little bit deeper to see, to take a closer look at exactly which prompts are in here, just to make sure that like, they're not necessarily like skewed to favor Lovable, just because this is pretty different from what we were seeing in Scrunch.

So there are still some things that I want to kind of dive into before I concretely say that this should be our baseline, but in terms of like the actual, in terms of actually like how the product works, it's like you have the position and sentiment and citation share, and then you're also able to see what's being cited.
So this 9 plus best no code, we're seeing, yeah, we're seeing some of the guides pages, so that's, yeah, which is, yeah, which does match what I was seeing.
I the LLM referrals data coming from the Looker dashboard.
So, yeah, so I can definitely share this with you once we have sorted out all of the permissions.
But, yeah, this is what we'll be using moving forward for AI visibility reporting.
But, again, yeah, I just want to get a little bit more context on how the prompts were developed just because I wasn't involved in that process.
But then I will make sure that it's set up for you all.
any questions or anything you want me to kind of dive into a little bit more?
Not from my side.
Just let us know when you want us to have, like, a look around that and feedback or if you need any input on the prompts.
Sorry, I didn't quite touch that.
What about the prompts or getting?
Let us know when you want us to have a look around the platform.
And also if there's any questions you have around the prompts.
Just last time.
Okay, perfect.
Perfect.
Yeah, what I might do is honestly just, yeah, export these and categorize them.
Yeah, I obviously don't want to force you to look through originating three prompts.
I can do a lot of that heavy lifting, but, and then I can just kind of sort of summarize for you what we're tracking.
And then maybe we can discuss if there are any places where, any areas that we should be tracking that maybe we're not.
Maybe I didn't catch this, but like, how were these prompts chosen now?
Yeah, so we have, so typically like we have a workflow that determines what the best prompts are for tracking, but essentially it's based on the, the context of the company.
So that's in here and then the competitors, but also, so we have basically like we have all of these like artifacts that they're just like, we use the same artifacts that we use.
We used to produce like the guides content and the templates content to develop the prompts to track.
So it's a lot of it, it's their prompts designed to tap into what maybe your standard audience personas are searching for.
It's trying to account for maybe like where competitors are performing.
So it's sort of a mix of things, but ultimately it's rooted in the company, the audience persona, competitors, and also just like overall business vision.
So even just like where you're going and like what the standard categories are.
So it's a mix, but I do still want to, just because I wasn't involved in putting this together, I still just want to take another look, a little closer look.
But I just wanted to give you a little review.
Okay.
Okay.
Interesting.
Oh, sorry.
Did you have something else you wanted?
Okay.
Cool.
Yeah.
mean, I think that was...

Something else.

Let me just pull that up.

And yeah, and then also at a certain point, like we were thinking, if there's ever more than 10 on a page, then at that point we'll implement pagination.
Hmm.

I can ask, do you want to tag Tom, the SEO guy we have in-house to review that as well?
Yeah, for sure.

Cool.

Okay.

Yeah.
Purchases is the, um, is like the main thing we need to drive.
I see, um, I see like impressions and clicks is, uh,

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content cadence, performance, and new AI visibility tools.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=139.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Cadence Clarified:** Guides will publish at 25/week (paid for 10) to quickly capture \~380 high-value keywords, then revert to 10/week. A 5-template backlog from an engineering publishing error will be cleared this week.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=139.0)
  - [**Template Strategy Shift:** January template purchases declined MoM. The strategy will shift from pure SEO to building templates for robust projects (e.g., e-commerce) that require a paid subscription, moving beyond projects achievable with free credits.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=557.0)
  - [**New AI Visibility Tool:** GrowthX is deploying "Check That," a new platform to track LLM visibility. Megan will validate its prompt list to ensure it's an accurate baseline before granting Lovable access.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=746.0)

## Topics

### Content Cadence & Backlog

  - [**Guides:** Publishing at 25/week (paid for 10) to quickly capture \~380 high-value keywords. The cadence will revert to 10/week once the keyword list is exhausted.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=139.0)
  - [**Templates:** A 5-template backlog from last week will be published this week. The delay was caused by an engineering publishing workflow error, not a content creation issue.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)

### January Performance Review

  - [**Guides:** Clicks surged 248% MoM, validating the high-volume publishing strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)
  - [**Templates:** Purchases declined MoM. This is specific to templates, as overall platform activity increased in January.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=459.0)
  - [**LLM Referrals:** Strong across the board, especially for guides.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)
  - [**Primary Metric:** Organic purchases are the key metric for reporting.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=1290.0)

### New Template Strategy

  - [**Problem:** The current SEO-driven template strategy may not align with user needs or drive conversions.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=604.0)
  - [**Goal:** Balance SEO with templates that drive paid subscriptions.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=604.0)
  - [**Focus:** Build templates for robust projects (e.g., e-commerce) that require a paid plan, not simple projects achievable with free credits.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=664.0)
  - [**Action:** Sam will provide data on what users are building to inform the new strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=604.0)

### New AI Visibility Tool: "Check That"

  - [**Purpose:** A new platform (replacing Scrunch) to track LLM visibility (position, sentiment, citation share).](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=746.0)
  - [**Status:** Megan will validate the prompt list to ensure it's an accurate baseline before granting Lovable access.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=886.0)
  - [**Prompt Selection:** Prompts are based on company context, competitors, audience personas, and business vision.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=1047.0)

### Template Page UI Preview

  - [**Feature:** A preview of the new template category filters was shared.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=1160.0)
  - [**Feedback:** The category buttons have inconsistent widths and should be made uniform.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=1240.0)
  - [**Future Feature:** Pagination will be added when a category exceeds 10 templates.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=1201.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Megan:**](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
      - [Ensure the 5-template backlog is published this week.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
      - [Flag the inconsistent button widths on the template page UI preview for the engineering team.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=1240.0)
      - [Validate the "Check That" prompt list and grant Lovable access.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=886.0)
      - [CC Tom (Lovable SEO) on the template page UI preview for review.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=1225.0)
  - [**Sam:**](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=604.0)
      - [Provide data on what users are building to inform the new template strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=604.0)
  - [**All:**](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=68.0)
      - [Schedule an in-person meeting for next Monday during Sam's visit.](https://fathom.video/share/qLmwaShfSHMbBSDAvoeChsqYyz8Zp5Ug?tab=summary&timestamp=68.0)

action_items.description: Publish 5 backlog templates + 10 this week; notify Sam/Johanna
Analyze Lovable usage to recommend premium template types to Megan
Export/categorize Check That prompts; share summary w/ Sam/Johanna; request prompt input
Email Templates category preview to Sam/Johanna; CC Tom; request SEO review
Flag uneven category widths to eng; fix
